- [ ] `recall.sh "config memory"` returns this entry
- [ ] Entry type is pattern

## Validation
- [ ] Single entry replaces the need to search 9 decision entries for this doctrine
- [ ] Tags include: security, config, pattern, injection, sanitization

## Files
- .beads/memory/knowledge.jsonl (written by hook automatically)

{"id":"bdcompound-7ji.3","title":"Fix PATTERN type gap: add guidance to use PATTERN: prefix explicitly","description":"## What

Add a PATTERN knowledge entry (and update CLAUDE.md guidance if appropriate) about when to use PATTERN: type explicitly vs burying patterns in DECISION/LEARNED entries.

## Context

The retro found 0 explicit PATTERN: type entries this week despite 7 entries auto-tagged 'pattern' from content. The tags come from keyword detection in memory-capture.sh, but the entry type stayed 'decision' or 'learned'. This makes it harder to filter patterns specifically and defeats the purpose of having distinct knowledge types.

The six types each have a purpose:
- LEARNED: insight from debugging/surprise behavior — one-time discovery
- DECISION: what was chosen and why — specific choice with rationale
- FACT: hard constraint, version req, environment detail — static truth
- PATTERN: recurring convention or idiom — should be reused everywhere
- INVESTIGATION: root cause analysis
- DEVIATION: auto-fix outside bead scope

When something is worth reusing as a convention (not just recording a one-time choice), it should be PATTERN.

## What

1. Add this as a PATTERN entry itself:

```bash
bd comments add bdcompound-7ji "PATTERN: Use PATTERN: type when capturing something meant to be reused as a convention — not just recording a one-time decision. Signal: if you'd want a future agent to do the same thing by default, it's a PATTERN. If you're recording why you made a specific choice, it's a DECISION. Examples of PATTERN: 'always XML-wrap injected content', 'use grep -iF for safe search'. Examples of DECISION: 'chose PostgreSQL over SQLite for this project', 'dropped filesystem scanning due to security risk'."
```

2. Optionally update the knowledge type descriptions in CLAUDE.md or hooks-system.md rule to add a 'When to use PATTERN vs DECISION' note.

## Decisions

### Locked
- The knowledge entry itself must use PATTERN: type (meta-example)

### Discretion
- Whether to also update CLAUDE.md/hooks-system.md rule (assess if it adds clarity)

## Testing
- [ ] Entry is type 'pattern'
- [ ] `recall.sh "pattern type"` returns this entry

## Validation
- [ ] Entry clearly distinguishes PATTERN from DECISION with concrete examples

## Files
- .beads/memory/knowledge.jsonl
- .claude/rules/hooks-system.md (optional — only if adding a clarifying note)

## Dependencies
None — but semantically follows bead 1 and 2 (do those first to practice the pattern)","status":"open","priority":4,"issue_type":"task","owner":"roberto.mello@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-18T17:18:24Z","created_by":"Roberto Mello","updated_at":"2026-03-18T17:18:24Z"}

{"id":"bdcompound-7ji.5","title":"Document chore: commit convention for infra/config changes","description":"## What

Add a knowledge entry and optionally a CLAUDE.md note clarifying when to use chore: vs other conventional commit prefixes, specifically for the infra/config changes that tend to slip through as untyped.

## Context

The CNAME commit this week was untyped: 'add CNAME for lavra.dev custom domain' should have been 'chore: add CNAME for lavra.dev custom domain'. Low stakes individually, but untyped commits break changelog generation and git log --grep filtering.

The ambiguous cases are:
- DNS/domain changes → chore:
- CI config changes → chore: or ci: (either fine, ci: preferred)
- Dependency bumps → chore:
- Gitignore/gitattributes → chore:
- README-only → docs:
- Script/tool additions → chore: (if not user-facing) or feat: (if user-facing)

## What

Add a FACT entry:

```bash
bd comments add bdcompound-7ji "FACT: Conventional commit type for infra/config-only changes: use chore: for DNS records, CI config, gitignore, gitattributes, dependency bumps, non-user-facing scripts. Use ci: for CI-specific changes (either is fine but ci: is more discoverable). These are the cases most likely to slip through as untyped commits."
```

Optionally add a one-liner to CLAUDE.md global instructions if the untyped pattern recurs.

## Decisions

### Locked
- Entry type: FACT (concrete rule, not a design choice)

### Discretion
- Whether to add to CLAUDE.md (only worth it if this is a recurring mistake)

## Testing
- [ ] Entry searchable via `recall.sh "chore commit"`

## Validation
- [ ] Tags include: git, commit, convention, chore

## Files
- .beads/memory/knowledge.jsonl
- ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md (optional)

## Dependencies

{"id":"bdcompound-7ji.4","title":"Automate docs accuracy check in pre-release","description":"## What

Add a script check and CI gate that cross-references CATALOG.md against actual command files in plugins/lavra/commands/, so ghost commands and missing commands are caught automatically rather than by manual inspection.

## Context

During this week's docs work, the catalog was found to have:
- 7 ghost commands (listed in catalog but file doesn't exist): /lfg, /lavra-compound, /create-agent-skill, /deploy-docs, /release-docs, /resolve-pr-parallel, /resolve-todo-parallel
- 5 missing commands (file exists but not in catalog): /lavra-ship, /lavra-qa, /lavra-retro, /lavra-learn, /lavra-ceo-review
- Wrong counts ('24 core' when actual is 23)

The release rules (github-release.md) now include a manual check step, but an automated script would catch this earlier and without relying on human discipline.

## What

Extend scripts/pre-release-check.sh with a command catalog accuracy check:

1. Extract command names listed in CATALOG.md (parse the table rows, extract /command-name entries)
2. List actual command files: ls plugins/lavra/commands/*.md + plugins/lavra/commands/optional/*.md
3. Compare: report commands in catalog but missing as files (ghosts), and files missing from catalog
4. Fail pre-release check if any ghosts found (file must exist for a command to be listed)
5. Warn (not fail) for missing from catalog (new commands may be intentionally unlisted during dev)

Also add the same check to .github/workflows/test-installation.yml verify-release job to keep them in sync (per the SYNC: convention both files share).

## Decisions

### Locked
- Ghost commands (in catalog, no file) = hard failure — never ship docs for commands that don't exist
- Missing from catalog (file exists, not in catalog) = warning only — may be intentional during dev
- Must keep pre-release-check.sh and CI yaml in sync (they have SYNC: comments pointing to each other)
- Use bash/grep/awk only — no new dependencies

### Discretion
- Exact parsing approach for CATALOG.md (grep for /lavra- pattern in table rows is simplest)
- Whether to check PLATFORMS.md feature flags (harder to automate, lower value — probably skip)

## Testing
- [ ] Script detects a ghost command when one is temporarily added to CATALOG.md
- [ ] Script detects a missing command when a new .md file is added to commands/ without updating catalog
- [ ] CI job runs the check and fails on ghost commands
- [ ] pre-release-check.sh and CI check use identical logic

## Validation
- [ ] `bash scripts/pre-release-check.sh` fails if CATALOG.md lists a command with no corresponding .md file
- [ ] `bash scripts/pre-release-check.sh` warns (exit 0) if a command file exists but isn't in CATALOG.md
- [ ] Both files have updated SYNC: comments referencing each other

## Files
- scripts/pre-release-check.sh
- .github/workflows/test-installation.yml

## Dependencies

{"description":"## Vision
Lavra owns its data in .lavra/ — a dedicated directory with its own .gitignore and .gitattributes — rather than writing into the beads-owned .beads/ directory. This enables clean separation between the two tools and makes Lavra installable on projects that don't use beads at all.

## Requirements
1. .lavra/ directory with .gitignore and .gitattributes at folder level (not subdirectory level)
2. .lavra/memory/ for knowledge.jsonl, recall.sh, knowledge-db.sh
3. .lavra/config/ for lavra.json, project-setup.md, codebase-profile.md
4. .lavra/retros/ for retro JSON snapshots
5. All hooks hard-recoded from .beads/ to .lavra/ paths (no fallback/dual-path)
6. Installer auto-migrates existing .beads/{memory,config,retros} data to .lavra/ on detection
7. Migration is copy + warn (originals left in place, user deletes after verification)
8. All commands, scripts, docs, CI updated to reference .lavra/ paths

## Non-Requirements
- No dual-path fallback logic in hooks
- No environment variable for LAVRA_DIR
- No interactive migration prompt (auto-detect + copy is silent with a warning)
- Does NOT touch .beads/ issue/comment/backup data (owned by beads CLI)

## Locked Decisions
- Folder-level .lavra/.gitignore and .lavra/.gitattributes (paths relative to .lavra/ root)
- Hard recode .beads/ → .lavra/ everywhere (no fallback detection in hooks)
- Auto-migrate on install: copy .beads/memory → .lavra/memory, .beads/config → .lavra/config, .beads/retros → .lavra/retros; leave originals with printed warning
- .lavra/ is committed to git (knowledge.jsonl, config, retros committed; SQLite cache and session-state.md gitignored)
- Implementation: one epic, sequential phase beads, each with tests and verification

## Agent Discretion
- Exact wording of the migration warning message
- Whether .lavra-version file lives at .lavra/ root or .lavra/memory/
- Order of operations within each phase bead

## Deferred
- OpenCode plugin.ts internal path changes (if the TS plugin has hardcoded .beads/ refs beyond what hooks handle)
- Cortex Code installer path updates (low priority, follow-on)

## Phases
- bdcompound-63c.2: Phase 1 — .lavra/ skeleton and provision-memory update
- bdcompound-63c.1: Phase 2 — Recode hooks from .beads/ to .lavra/
- bdcompound-63c.3: Phase 3 — Installer migration
- bdcompound-63c.4: Phase 4 — Update commands and scripts
- bdcompound-63c.5: Phase 5 — Update docs, CI, CLAUDE.md rules, release checklist
- bdcompound-63c.6: Phase 6 — Smoke tests and version bump"}

{"description":"## What
Update provision-memory.sh to create and manage a .lavra/ directory structure instead of writing into .beads/. Create a folder-level .lavra/.gitignore and .lavra/.gitattributes (NOT subdirectory-level). This is the foundation — all other phases depend on this structure being correct.

## Context
provision-memory.sh is at plugins/lavra/hooks/provision-memory.sh. It currently:
- Creates .beads/memory/ with knowledge.jsonl, recall.sh, knowledge-db.sh, .gitattributes, .gitignore, .lavra-version
- Creates .beads/config/ with lavra.json
- Runs git add on all .beads/memory/ files
- Validates project .gitignore for .beads/ patterns (warns if present)

The new structure moves ALL of this to .lavra/, with a single .gitignore and .gitattributes at .lavra/ root (not .lavra/memory/).

## New .lavra/ Directory Structure
```
.lavra/
├── .gitignore        (NEW: folder-level, replaces .beads/memory/.gitignore)
├── .gitattributes    (NEW: folder-level, replaces .beads/memory/.gitattributes)
├── .lavra-version    (MOVED: was .beads/memory/.lavra-version)
├── memory/
│   ├── knowledge.jsonl
│   ├── knowledge.archive.jsonl
│   ├── knowledge.db           (gitignored via .lavra/.gitignore)
│   ├── knowledge.db-journal   (gitignored)
│   ├── knowledge.db-wal       (gitignored)
│   ├── knowledge.db-shm       (gitignored)
│   ├── session-state.md       (gitignored)
│   ├── recall.sh
│   └── knowledge-db.sh
├── config/
│   ├── lavra.json
│   ├── project-setup.md       (committed when created)
│   └── codebase-profile.md    (committed when created)
└── retros/
    └── {date}.json            (committed)
```

## .lavra/.gitignore content (new, folder-level)
```
# SQLite FTS cache (rebuilt from knowledge.jsonl on first use)
memory/knowledge.db
memory/knowledge.db-journal
memory/knowledge.db-wal
memory/knowledge.db-shm

# Ephemeral session state (survives compaction, recalled once, then deleted)
memory/session-state.md
```
Note: paths are relative to .lavra/ root, so they use memory/ prefix.

## .lavra/.gitattributes content (new, folder-level)
```
memory/knowledge.jsonl merge=union
memory/knowledge.archive.jsonl merge=union
```
Note: paths are relative to .lavra/ root.

## Decisions

### Locked
- Folder-level .gitignore and .gitattributes at .lavra/ root (NOT .lavra/memory/)
- .lavra-version marker lives at .lavra/.lavra-version (NOT .lavra/memory/)
- Hard path to .lavra/ — no .beads/ fallback
- .lavra/ contents committed to git; SQLite cache and session-state.md gitignored
- mkdir for .lavra/retros/ must also be created (currently .beads/retros/ is created by lavra-retro command, but installer should pre-create it)

### Discretion
- Exact wording of the printed output messages ("Memory system configured" etc.)
- Whether to create knowledge.archive.jsonl as empty file or let it be created on first rotation

## Specific Changes to provision-memory.sh
1. Line 18: `MEMORY_DIR="$PROJECT_DIR/.beads/memory"` → `MEMORY_DIR="$PROJECT_DIR/.lavra/memory"`
2. Line 72: `CONFIG_DIR="$PROJECT_DIR/.beads/config"` → `CONFIG_DIR="$PROJECT_DIR/.lavra/config"`
3. Add: `LAVRA_DIR="$PROJECT_DIR/.lavra"` variable at top of provision function
4. Add: `mkdir -p "$PROJECT_DIR/.lavra/retros"` in provisioning block
5. .gitattributes: write to `$LAVRA_DIR/.gitattributes` (NOT `$MEMORY_DIR/.gitattributes`) with `memory/` prefixed paths
6. .gitignore: write to `$LAVRA_DIR/.gitignore` (NOT `$MEMORY_DIR/.gitignore`) with `memory/` prefixed paths
7. .lavra-version: write to `$LAVRA_DIR/.lavra-version` (NOT `$MEMORY_DIR/.lavra-version`)
8. Lines 97-107: Update gitignore validation — change regex from `\.beads/` to `\.lavra/` in the project .gitignore warning check
9. Lines 135-143: Update git add to stage:
   - .lavra/.gitattributes
   - .lavra/.gitignore
   - .lavra/.lavra-version
   - .lavra/memory/knowledge.jsonl
   - .lavra/memory/recall.sh
   - .lavra/memory/knowledge-db.sh
10. Update all echo messages to reference .lavra/ not .beads/

## Testing
- [ ] Run provision-memory.sh on a fresh temp project: verify .lavra/ created with correct structure
- [ ] Verify .lavra/.gitignore exists at folder root (not .lavra/memory/.gitignore)
- [ ] Verify .lavra/.gitattributes exists at folder root (not .lavra/memory/.gitattributes)
- [ ] Verify .lavra/.lavra-version exists at folder root (not .lavra/memory/)
- [ ] Verify .lavra/memory/knowledge.jsonl, recall.sh, knowledge-db.sh exist
- [ ] Verify .lavra/config/lavra.json exists with correct JSON
- [ ] Verify .lavra/retros/ directory exists
- [ ] Verify NO .beads/memory/ directory was created
- [ ] Verify git add commands stage the right files (check git status)
- [ ] Run provision-memory.sh twice — should be idempotent (no duplicate files/dirs)
- [ ] Test gitignore validation: create a project with .lavra/ in .gitignore — confirm warning fires

## Validation
- [ ] .lavra/.gitignore uses memory/ prefix paths (not bare knowledge.db)
- [ ] .lavra/.gitattributes uses memory/ prefix paths (not bare knowledge.jsonl)
- [ ] No .beads/memory/ references remain in provision-memory.sh
- [ ] No .beads/config/ references remain in provision-memory.sh
- [ ] Version string "0.7.0" still matches LAVRA_VERSION in auto-recall.sh (just path changed)

## Files
- plugins/lavra/hooks/provision-memory.sh (primary)

## Dependencies
None — this is Phase 1, the foundation.

## References
- File: plugins/lavra/hooks/provision-memory.sh — full file to be updated
- Brainstorm: bdcompound-63c — locked decisions on gitignore/gitattributes placement"}

{"description":"## What
Update all installer and uninstaller scripts to provision .lavra/ instead of .beads/memory/, and add auto-migration logic that copies existing .beads/ Lavra data to .lavra/ on detection. Covers Claude, OpenCode, Gemini, and Cortex installers.

## Context
The installers live in installers/. Each platform installer calls provision-memory.sh (already updated in Phase 1) to set up the memory system. The installer also creates config directories, prints messaging, and handles the uninstall side. The new migration logic must detect if existing Lavra data lives in .beads/ and copy it to .lavra/ before proceeding.

## Migration Logic (for all installers)

Detection and copy block to add to each installer (after provision-memory.sh call):

```bash
# Migrate existing .beads/ Lavra data to .lavra/ if present
_migrate_beads_to_lavra() {
  local TARGET="$1"
  local migrated=0

  if [ -d "$TARGET/.beads/memory" ] && [ -f "$TARGET/.beads/memory/knowledge.jsonl" ]; then
    cp -rn "$TARGET/.beads/memory/." "$TARGET/.lavra/memory/" 2>/dev/null || true
    migrated=1
  fi

  if [ -d "$TARGET/.beads/config" ] && [ -f "$TARGET/.beads/config/lavra.json" ]; then
    cp -rn "$TARGET/.beads/config/lavra.json" "$TARGET/.lavra/config/" 2>/dev/null || true
    # Only copy lavra-owned config files, not beads-owned ones
    [ -f "$TARGET/.beads/config/project-setup.md" ] && cp -n "$TARGET/.beads/config/project-setup.md" "$TARGET/.lavra/config/" 2>/dev/null || true
    [ -f "$TARGET/.beads/config/codebase-profile.md" ] && cp -n "$TARGET/.beads/config/codebase-profile.md" "$TARGET/.lavra/config/" 2>/dev/null || true
    migrated=1
  fi

  if [ -d "$TARGET/.beads/retros" ]; then
    cp -rn "$TARGET/.beads/retros/." "$TARGET/.lavra/retros/" 2>/dev/null || true
    migrated=1
  fi

  if [ "$migrated" -eq 1 ]; then
    echo ""
    echo "[!] Old Lavra data found in .beads/ — copied to .lavra/"
    echo "    .beads/memory/, .beads/config/ (Lavra files), .beads/retros/ preserved."
    echo "    Safe to delete after verifying .lavra/ data is intact."
    echo ""
  fi
}
```

Notes on migration logic:
- Use `cp -rn` (no-clobber) to not overwrite existing .lavra/ files if already present
- Only copy Lavra-owned config files (lavra.json, project-setup.md, codebase-profile.md) — NOT beads-owned files
- Do NOT copy .beads/backup/ (owned by beads CLI)
- Do NOT delete .beads/ originals (user deletes after verification)
- Call _migrate_beads_to_lavra AFTER provision-memory.sh runs (so .lavra/ dirs exist)

## Specific Changes Per Installer

### install-claude.sh
1. Line 155: `mkdir -p "$TARGET/.beads/config"` → `mkdir -p "$TARGET/.lavra/config"` (provision-memory.sh handles this, but belt-and-suspenders)
2. Lines 474-475: Update messaging:
   - "Knowledge stored at .lavra/memory/knowledge.jsonl"
   - "Search: .lavra/memory/recall.sh 'keyword'"
3. Add: Call _migrate_beads_to_lavra after provision-memory.sh call

### install-opencode.sh, install-gemini.sh, install-cortex.sh
Same pattern:
1. Update any config directory creation to .lavra/config
2. Update messaging references from .beads/ to .lavra/
3. Add: Call _migrate_beads_to_lavra after provision-memory.sh call

### uninstall-claude.sh
1. Update lines 13-14, 224-225, 230: messaging about .beads/ → .lavra/ (what gets preserved)
2. Do NOT auto-delete .lavra/ — preserve knowledge (same policy as .beads/)
3. Add note: "To fully remove Lavra data: rm -rf .lavra/"

### uninstall-opencode.sh, uninstall-gemini.sh, uninstall-cortex.sh
Update references from .beads/memory/recall.sh and .beads/memory/knowledge-db.sh to .lavra/ paths (lines 131-143 in opencode, 110-122 in gemini).

### scripts/test-installation.sh and scripts/test-features.sh
Update all path checks from .beads/config/ and .beads/memory/ to .lavra/ equivalents.

## Decisions

### Locked
- Copy + warn (not delete, not interactive prompt)
- Do NOT touch .beads/ issue/comment/backup data — only Lavra-owned files
- Migration fires automatically on install if .beads/memory/knowledge.jsonl detected
- Use cp -rn (no-clobber) to be safe on re-installs

### Discretion
- Exact warning message wording
- Whether to print migration details (which files were copied) or just a summary line

## Testing
- [ ] Fresh install test: create temp project with no .beads/. Run installer. Verify .lavra/memory/ created, no .beads/memory/ written by Lavra.
- [ ] Migration install test: create temp project with .beads/memory/knowledge.jsonl populated. Run installer. Verify .lavra/memory/knowledge.jsonl contains data, .beads/memory/ still intact, warning printed.
- [ ] Re-install test: run installer twice on a project that already has .lavra/. Verify no data duplication or overwrite.
- [ ] Config migration: verify lavra.json, project-setup.md, codebase-profile.md copied if present.
- [ ] Retro migration: create a .beads/retros/2026-01-01.json. Verify it appears in .lavra/retros/.
- [ ] Verify .beads/backup/ NOT touched by migration.
- [ ] Run smoke tests from github-release.md (will be updated in Phase 5) against updated installer.

## Validation
- [ ] `grep -r "\.beads/memory" installers/` returns zero results
- [ ] `grep -r "\.beads/config" installers/` returns only migration-detection code
- [ ] Warning message printed when .beads/memory/knowledge.jsonl detected
- [ ] No warning when fresh install (no .beads/memory/ present)
- [ ] .beads/ data preserved after migration

## Files
- installers/install-claude.sh (primary)
- installers/install-opencode.sh
- installers/install-gemini.sh
- installers/install-cortex.sh
- installers/uninstall-claude.sh
- installers/uninstall-opencode.sh
- installers/uninstall-gemini.sh
- installers/uninstall-cortex.sh
- scripts/test-installation.sh
- scripts/test-features.sh

## Dependencies
- bdcompound-63c.1 (Phase 2) — hooks must target .lavra/ before installer runs

## References
- File: installers/install-claude.sh lines 138-157 — provision-memory.sh integration
- File: installers/uninstall-opencode.sh lines 131-143 — cleanup paths
- Brainstorm: bdcompound-63c — copy + warn migration decision"}

```bash
bash ~/Documents/projects/lavra/install.sh /tmp/test-lavra-fresh 2>&1 | grep -A5 '\[!\] Warning'
# Must show data loss warning (now about .lavra/ not .beads/)
```

### Cleanup
```bash
rm -rf /tmp/test-lavra-fresh /tmp/test-lavra-migrate /tmp/test-lavra-opencode /tmp/test-lavra-gemini
cd ~/Documents/projects/lavra
```

## Final Grep Validation (must all return zero results)
```bash
grep -r "\.beads/memory" plugins/lavra/
grep -r "\.beads/config" plugins/lavra/
grep -r "\.beads/retros" plugins/lavra/
grep -r "\.beads/memory" installers/
grep -r "\.beads/config" installers/
grep -r "\.beads/memory" .claude/rules/
grep -r "\.beads/memory" site/src/content/docs/
```

Exceptions allowed:
- `.beads/backup/` references in lavra-ship.md (beads CLI owned)
- `.beads/` refs in releases/v0.7.0.md (historical)
- Migration detection code in installers that checks for .beads/memory/ to decide whether to migrate

## Version Bump
Update these 4 files to the next minor version (current: 0.7.0 → next: 0.8.0):
1. plugins/lavra/.claude-plugin/plugin.json
2. .claude-plugin/marketplace.json
3. LAVRA_VERSION constant in plugins/lavra/hooks/auto-recall.sh
4. Version string in plugins/lavra/hooks/provision-memory.sh (the `echo "X.Y.Z"` line)

Run pre-release-check.sh to verify all 4 match.

## Pre-Release Check
```bash
bash scripts/pre-release-check.sh
```
Must pass with zero failures.

## Decisions

### Locked
- Both fresh install AND migration scenarios must pass before tagging
- Version bump to 0.8.0 (next minor — this is a significant structural change)
- All 4 version locations must match

### Discretion
- Exact version number (0.8.0 vs 0.7.1) — agent decides based on changelog impact

## Testing
All tests described above in the smoke test matrix.

## Validation
- [ ] All grep checks return zero results (with exceptions listed)
- [ ] Fresh install: .lavra/ root has .gitignore, .gitattributes, .lavra-version
- [ ] Fresh install: .lavra/memory/ has knowledge.jsonl, recall.sh, knowledge-db.sh
- [ ] Migration install: .lavra/ populated with .beads/ data, originals preserved
- [ ] Migration warning message printed
- [ ] OpenCode installer creates .lavra/memory/ correctly
- [ ] Gemini installer creates .lavra/memory/ correctly
- [ ] .lavra/ gitignore warning fires when .lavra/ is in project .gitignore
- [ ] pre-release-check.sh passes
- [ ] All 4 version files updated and consistent

## Files
- plugins/lavra/.claude-plugin/plugin.json (version bump)
- .claude-plugin/marketplace.json (version bump)
- plugins/lavra/hooks/auto-recall.sh (version constant)
- plugins/lavra/hooks/provision-memory.sh (version string)

## Dependencies
- bdcompound-63c.5 (Phase 5) must complete first

## References
- File: .claude/rules/github-release.md — smoke test template (updated in Phase 5)
- Brainstorm: bdcompound-63c — version bump rationale"}

{"description":"## What
Update provision-memory.sh to create and manage a .lavra/ directory structure instead of writing into .beads/. Create a folder-level .lavra/.gitignore and .lavra/.gitattributes (NOT subdirectory-level). This is the foundation — all other phases depend on this structure being correct.

## Context
provision-memory.sh is at plugins/lavra/hooks/provision-memory.sh. It currently:
- Creates .beads/memory/ with knowledge.jsonl, recall.sh, knowledge-db.sh, .gitattributes, .gitignore, .lavra-version
- Creates .beads/config/ with lavra.json
- Runs git add on all .beads/memory/ files
- Validates project .gitignore for .beads/ patterns (warns if present)

The new structure moves ALL of this to .lavra/, with a single .gitignore and .gitattributes at .lavra/ root (not .lavra/memory/).

## New .lavra/ Directory Structure
```
.lavra/
├── .gitignore        (NEW: folder-level, replaces .beads/memory/.gitignore)
├── .gitattributes    (NEW: folder-level, replaces .beads/memory/.gitattributes)
├── .lavra-version    (MOVED: was .beads/memory/.lavra-version)
├── memory/
│   ├── knowledge.jsonl
│   ├── knowledge.archive.jsonl
│   ├── knowledge.db           (gitignored via .lavra/.gitignore)
│   ├── knowledge.db-journal   (gitignored)
│   ├── knowledge.db-wal       (gitignored)
│   ├── knowledge.db-shm       (gitignored)
│   ├── session-state.md       (gitignored)
│   ├── recall.sh
│   └── knowledge-db.sh
├── config/
│   ├── lavra.json
│   ├── project-setup.md       (committed when created)
│   └── codebase-profile.md    (committed when created)
└── retros/
    └── {date}.json            (committed)
```

## .lavra/.gitignore content (new, folder-level)
```
# SQLite FTS cache (rebuilt from knowledge.jsonl on first use)
memory/knowledge.db
memory/knowledge.db-journal
memory/knowledge.db-wal
memory/knowledge.db-shm

# Ephemeral session state (survives compaction, recalled once, then deleted)
memory/session-state.md
```
Note: paths are relative to .lavra/ root, so they use memory/ prefix.

## .lavra/.gitattributes content (new, folder-level)
```
memory/knowledge.jsonl merge=union
memory/knowledge.archive.jsonl merge=union
```
Note: paths are relative to .lavra/ root. CRITICAL: pattern must match the subpath — if gitattributes is at .lavra/ root, it must say `memory/knowledge.jsonl`, not bare `knowledge.jsonl`.

## Decisions

### Locked
- Folder-level .gitignore and .gitattributes at .lavra/ root (NOT .lavra/memory/)
- .lavra-version marker lives at .lavra/.lavra-version (NOT .lavra/memory/)
- Hard path to .lavra/ — no .beads/ fallback
- .lavra/ contents committed to git; SQLite cache and session-state.md gitignored
- mkdir for .lavra/retros/ must also be created (currently .beads/retros/ is created by lavra-retro command, but installer should pre-create it)

### Discretion
- Exact wording of the printed output messages ("Memory system configured" etc.)
- Whether to create knowledge.archive.jsonl as empty file or let it be created on first rotation

## Specific Changes to provision-memory.sh
1. Line 18: `MEMORY_DIR="$PROJECT_DIR/.beads/memory"` → `MEMORY_DIR="$PROJECT_DIR/.lavra/memory"`
2. Line 72: `CONFIG_DIR="$PROJECT_DIR/.beads/config"` → `CONFIG_DIR="$PROJECT_DIR/.lavra/config"`
3. Add: `LAVRA_DIR="$PROJECT_DIR/.lavra"` variable at top of provision function
4. Add: `mkdir -p "$PROJECT_DIR/.lavra/retros"` in provisioning block
5. .gitattributes: write to `$LAVRA_DIR/.gitattributes` (NOT `$MEMORY_DIR/.gitattributes`) with `memory/` prefixed paths
6. .gitignore: write to `$LAVRA_DIR/.gitignore` (NOT `$MEMORY_DIR/.gitignore`) with `memory/` prefixed paths
7. .lavra-version: write to `$LAVRA_DIR/.lavra-version` (NOT `$MEMORY_DIR/.lavra-version`)
8. Lines 97-107: Update gitignore validation — change regex from `\.beads/` to `\.lavra/` in the project .gitignore warning check. Also update warning message text — it currently says "beads issues, comments, and knowledge" but should say "Lavra knowledge and config"
9. Lines 135-143: Update git add to stage:
   - .lavra/.gitattributes
   - .lavra/.gitignore
   - .lavra/.lavra-version
   - .lavra/memory/knowledge.jsonl
   - .lavra/memory/recall.sh
   - .lavra/memory/knowledge-db.sh
   CRITICAL: This staging block MUST be updated — if it still stages .beads/memory/ paths, git history will diverge from the new directory location
10. Update all echo messages to reference .lavra/ not .beads/

## Research Insights

**Critical from architecture review:**
- The `git add -f` staging block in provision-memory.sh (lines 135-143) MUST be updated or git history will point to wrong paths. This is the single highest-risk missed change — easy to overlook because it's at the end of the function.
- The .gitattributes `.gitattributes` scoping: git uses the file in the closest ancestor. `.lavra/.gitattributes` with `memory/knowledge.jsonl` correctly governs `.lavra/memory/knowledge.jsonl`. The path prefix is critical — bare `knowledge.jsonl` would NOT match.

**Critical from pattern review:**
- `.lavra-version` naming is correct. Do not rename it. Keep namespacing intent.
- The `.beads/` gitignore safety check (lines 95-131) currently warns when `.beads/` is in the project .gitignore. After migration, it must ALSO check for `.lavra/` — the new data loss risk. Add a parallel check for `\.lavra/?` pattern.

**From deployment review:**
- After `cp -rn` in Phase 3, there may be a stale `.lavra/memory/.gitattributes` from the migration copy. This would be redundant but not harmful (git uses the most-specific file). The canonical file is `.lavra/.gitattributes`. Do not write a `.gitattributes` to `.lavra/memory/` from provision-memory.sh.

## Testing
- [ ] Run provision-memory.sh on a fresh temp project: verify .lavra/ created with correct structure
- [ ] Verify .lavra/.gitignore exists at folder root (not .lavra/memory/.gitignore)
- [ ] Verify .lavra/.gitattributes exists at folder root (not .lavra/memory/.gitattributes)
- [ ] Verify .lavra/.gitattributes contains `memory/knowledge.jsonl merge=union` (with memory/ prefix)
- [ ] Verify .lavra/.lavra-version exists at folder root (not .lavra/memory/)
- [ ] Verify .lavra/memory/knowledge.jsonl, recall.sh, knowledge-db.sh exist
- [ ] Verify .lavra/config/lavra.json exists with correct JSON
- [ ] Verify .lavra/retros/ directory exists
- [ ] Verify NO .beads/memory/ directory was created
- [ ] Verify git add commands stage .lavra/ files (check git status — must show .lavra/...)
- [ ] Run provision-memory.sh twice — should be idempotent (no duplicate files/dirs)
- [ ] Test gitignore validation: project with .lavra/ in .gitignore — confirm warning fires
- [ ] Run `git check-attr merge .lavra/memory/knowledge.jsonl` after install — must return `merge=union`

## Validation
- [ ] .lavra/.gitignore uses memory/ prefix paths (not bare knowledge.db)
- [ ] .lavra/.gitattributes uses memory/ prefix paths (not bare knowledge.jsonl)
- [ ] No .beads/memory/ references remain in provision-memory.sh
- [ ] No .beads/config/ references remain in provision-memory.sh
- [ ] git add block stages .lavra/ paths, not .beads/ paths
- [ ] .lavra/ gitignore check covers both .beads/ (old warning, can be removed) and .lavra/ (new)
- [ ] Version string "0.7.0" still matches LAVRA_VERSION in auto-recall.sh (just path changed)

## Files
- plugins/lavra/hooks/provision-memory.sh (primary)

## Dependencies
None — this is Phase 1, the foundation.

## References
- File: plugins/lavra/hooks/provision-memory.sh — full file to be updated
- Brainstorm: bdcompound-63c — locked decisions on gitignore/gitattributes placement
- Architecture review: git add -f staging block is the highest-risk missed change
- Pattern review: .lavra-version naming is correct, keep it
- Deployment review: no .gitattributes in .lavra/memory/ from provision-memory.sh"}

{"description":"## What
Recode all hook scripts to use .lavra/ paths instead of .beads/ paths. Three files change (auto-recall.sh, memory-capture.sh, check-memory.sh + recall.sh template). Two files need zero changes (subagent-wrapup.sh, knowledge-db.sh).

## Context
Hook scripts live in plugins/lavra/hooks/. After Phase 1 updates provision-memory.sh, these hooks still point to .beads/ paths and will fail. This phase updates the runtime hooks so the installed system works end-to-end.

Files that need changes:
- auto-recall.sh (SessionStart hook) — references .beads/ at lines 33, 41, 64-65
- memory-capture.sh (PostToolUse hook) — references .beads/memory/ at line 89
- check-memory.sh (optional check script) — references .beads/memory/ at lines 37, 97
- recall.sh (template search script, installed to .lavra/memory/) — references .beads/memory/ at lines 3, 14

Files that need ZERO changes:
- subagent-wrapup.sh — has NO .beads/ references (confirmed)
- knowledge-db.sh — all paths are passed as parameters, fully agnostic (confirmed)

## Specific Changes

### auto-recall.sh
- Line 33: `"$PROJECT_DIR/.beads"` → `"$PROJECT_DIR/.lavra"` (bootstrap existence check)
- Line 41: `"$PROJECT_DIR/.beads/memory"` → `"$PROJECT_DIR/.lavra/memory"` (MEMORY_DIR init)
- Add: `LAVRA_DIR="$PROJECT_DIR/.lavra"` variable after MEMORY_DIR init
- Line 64: `"$PROJECT_DIR/.beads/memory"` → `"$PROJECT_DIR/.lavra/memory"` (version file directory)
- Line 65: `"$MEMORY_DIR/.lavra-version"` → `"$LAVRA_DIR/.lavra-version"` (version file is now at .lavra/ root, not .lavra/memory/)
  CRITICAL: .lavra-version moved to .lavra/ root (per Phase 1). MUST use LAVRA_DIR not MEMORY_DIR for this path.
- Line 199 (source annotation in system message): `source=".beads/memory/knowledge.jsonl"` → `source=".lavra/memory/knowledge.jsonl"` — user-visible in model context, confusing if stale.
- Session state path: auto-resolves since it uses $MEMORY_DIR — no explicit change needed

### memory-capture.sh
- Line 89: `"${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR:-${CWD:-.}}/.beads/memory"` → `"${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR:-${CWD:-.}}/.lavra/memory"`
- All other references (KNOWLEDGE_FILE, knowledge.db paths) derive from MEMORY_DIR — auto-update once line 89 is changed

### check-memory.sh
- Line 37: update .beads/memory/ → .lavra/memory/
- Line 97: `MEMORY_DIR=".beads/memory"` → `MEMORY_DIR=".lavra/memory"` — THIS IS A FALLBACK HARDCODE not listed in original scope. MUST be caught.
  Note: check-memory.sh has a gate at line 38: `if [ ! -d ".beads" ]; then exit 0`. After migration Lavra works without beads. This gate is now architecturally incorrect (Lavra's memory doesn't need beads). Document this as a known limitation in a comment but do NOT change the gate in this phase — it would be a behavior change. File a follow-up issue.

### recall.sh (template, gets installed to .lavra/memory/recall.sh)
- Lines 3, 14: update .beads/memory/knowledge.jsonl → .lavra/memory/knowledge.jsonl
  Note: recall.sh is installed TO .lavra/memory/ by provision-memory.sh. Examine path resolution logic (how does it find knowledge.jsonl — relative or absolute?) before changing.

## Security Improvements (from security review)
- Add CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR validation in memory-capture.sh before constructing MEMORY_DIR path:
  ```bash
  if [[ -n "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR" ]]; then
    if [[ "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR" != /* ]] || \
       [[ ! -d "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/.lavra" && ! -d "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/.beads" ]]; then
      CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR=""  # fall through to CWD
    fi
  fi
  ```
  This prevents write-redirection attacks if CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR env var is maliciously set.

## Decisions

### Locked
- Hard recode — no dual-path fallback, no env var
- subagent-wrapup.sh and knowledge-db.sh are NOT modified
- .lavra-version file is at .lavra/ root (not .lavra/memory/) — auto-recall.sh must use LAVRA_DIR for version check
- check-memory.sh gate (`.beads` existence check) is NOT changed in this phase — document as known limitation

### Discretion
- Whether to extract LAVRA_DIR as a separate variable or inline the path calculation (LAVRA_DIR variable is cleaner)
- Whether to apply the CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR security fix in this phase or as separate issue

## Testing
- [ ] Fresh install with updated hooks: verify SessionStart hook fires and injects knowledge from .lavra/memory/knowledge.jsonl
- [ ] Run memory-capture.sh manually with a bd comment — verify entry appended to .lavra/memory/knowledge.jsonl
- [ ] Verify .lavra/memory/knowledge.db created by knowledge-db.sh (called from auto-recall.sh)
- [ ] Verify auto-recall.sh reads .lavra/.lavra-version (not .lavra/memory/.lavra-version)
- [ ] Verify session-state.md at .lavra/memory/session-state.md (not .beads/memory/)
- [ ] grep -r "\.beads/" plugins/lavra/hooks/ should return zero results (excluding migration script)
- [ ] Verify source annotation in auto-recall.sh output shows .lavra/memory/ path (not .beads/memory/)

## Validation
- [ ] No .beads/ references remain in auto-recall.sh, memory-capture.sh, check-memory.sh, recall.sh
- [ ] check-memory.sh line 97 fallback MUST be updated (MEMORY_DIR=".lavra/memory")
- [ ] auto-recall.sh correctly reads version from .lavra/.lavra-version (LAVRA_DIR, not MEMORY_DIR)
- [ ] memory-capture.sh writes to .lavra/memory/knowledge.jsonl on a real bd comment

## Files
- plugins/lavra/hooks/auto-recall.sh
- plugins/lavra/hooks/memory-capture.sh
- plugins/lavra/hooks/check-memory.sh
- plugins/lavra/hooks/recall.sh

## Dependencies
- bdcompound-63c.2 (Phase 1) must complete first — defines the .lavra/ structure these hooks target

## References
- File: plugins/lavra/hooks/auto-recall.sh — version check at line 64-65 critical; source annotation line 199
- File: plugins/lavra/hooks/memory-capture.sh — MEMORY_DIR at line 89
- File: plugins/lavra/hooks/check-memory.sh — fallback hardcode at line 97 is easy to miss
- Brainstorm: bdcompound-63c — hard recode decision
- Security review: CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR should be validated before use
- Architecture review: source annotation line 199 in auto-recall.sh must be updated"}

{"description":"## What
Update all installer and uninstaller scripts to provision .lavra/ instead of .beads/memory/, and add auto-migration logic that copies existing .beads/ Lavra data to .lavra/ on detection. Covers Claude, OpenCode, Gemini, and Cortex installers. Includes security hardening and post-copy integrity verification.

## Context
The installers live in installers/. Each platform installer calls provision-memory.sh (already updated in Phase 1) to set up the memory system. The migration function must: detect existing Lavra data in .beads/, copy it safely to .lavra/, and exclude stale cached files.

## Migration Function (full spec)

```bash
migrate_beads_to_lavra() {
  local TARGET="$1"
  local migrated=0

  # Security: validate TARGET is absolute path with no traversal
  [[ "$TARGET" != /* ]] && { echo "[!] migrate: TARGET must be absolute path" >&2; return 1; }
  [[ "$TARGET" == *".."* ]] && { echo "[!] migrate: TARGET contains traversal sequences" >&2; return 1; }

  # Skip if .lavra/memory/knowledge.jsonl already has data (idempotent re-run protection)
  if [[ -s "$TARGET/.lavra/memory/knowledge.jsonl" ]]; then
    echo "  [i] .lavra/ already has knowledge data -- skipping migration"
    return 0
  fi

  if [ -d "$TARGET/.beads/memory" ] && [ -f "$TARGET/.beads/memory/knowledge.jsonl" ]; then
    # NOTE: provision_memory_dir MUST have run before this line.
    # It writes new recall.sh and knowledge-db.sh to .lavra/memory/.
    # cp -rn will not overwrite them (no-clobber). Order is load-bearing.
    # Exclude SQLite cache files — they may be in inconsistent state (open WAL)
    for f in "$TARGET/.beads/memory/"*; do
      fname=$(basename "$f")
      case "$fname" in
        *.db|*.db-wal|*.db-shm|*.db-journal|.gitattributes|.gitignore) continue ;;
        recall.sh|knowledge-db.sh) continue ;;  # new versions already written by provisioning
        *) cp -n "$f" "$TARGET/.lavra/memory/" 2>/dev/null || true ;;
      esac
    done
    migrated=1
  fi

  if [ -d "$TARGET/.beads/config" ] && [ -f "$TARGET/.beads/config/lavra.json" ]; then
    cp -n "$TARGET/.beads/config/lavra.json" "$TARGET/.lavra/config/" 2>/dev/null || true
    [ -f "$TARGET/.beads/config/project-setup.md" ] && cp -n "$TARGET/.beads/config/project-setup.md" "$TARGET/.lavra/config/" 2>/dev/null || true
    [ -f "$TARGET/.beads/config/codebase-profile.md" ] && cp -n "$TARGET/.beads/config/codebase-profile.md" "$TARGET/.lavra/config/" 2>/dev/null || true
    migrated=1
  fi

  if [ -d "$TARGET/.beads/retros" ]; then
    mkdir -p "$TARGET/.lavra/retros"
    # cp -rn on a directory: if destination exists, copies CONTENTS (not the dir itself)
    # mkdir -p ensures destination exists before copy
    cp -rn "$TARGET/.beads/retros/." "$TARGET/.lavra/retros/" 2>/dev/null || true
    migrated=1
  fi

  if [ "$migrated" -eq 1 ]; then
    # Post-copy integrity check for knowledge.jsonl
    SRC_LINES=$(wc -l < "$TARGET/.beads/memory/knowledge.jsonl" 2>/dev/null | tr -d ' ')
    DST_LINES=$(wc -l < "$TARGET/.lavra/memory/knowledge.jsonl" 2>/dev/null | tr -d ' ')
    if [[ "${SRC_LINES:-0}" -gt 0 ]] && [[ "${DST_LINES:-0}" -lt "${SRC_LINES:-0}" ]]; then
      echo "[!] Migration warning: .beads/memory/knowledge.jsonl has $SRC_LINES lines, .lavra/ has $DST_LINES" >&2
      echo "    Run: cp -f \"$TARGET/.beads/memory/knowledge.jsonl\" \"$TARGET/.lavra/memory/knowledge.jsonl\" to fix" >&2
    fi
    echo ""
    echo "[!] Old Lavra data found in .beads/ — copied to .lavra/"
    echo "    .beads/ data preserved. Safe to delete after verifying .lavra/ data is intact."
    echo "    To remove: rm -rf .beads/memory .beads/config/lavra.json .beads/retros"
    echo ""
  fi
}
```

IMPORTANT NOTES on the migration function:
1. Function name is `migrate_beads_to_lavra` (no underscore prefix — consistent with codebase snake_case convention)
2. SQLite files (.db, .db-wal, .db-shm, .db-journal) are EXCLUDED — they may have open WAL state
3. recall.sh and knowledge-db.sh are EXCLUDED — new versions already written by provision_memory_dir
4. Old .gitattributes and .gitignore are EXCLUDED — they would create stale subdirectory-level files that conflict with new .lavra/ root-level files
5. mkdir -p for retros destination MUST precede cp -rn (cp -rn behavior differs when dest doesn't exist)
6. Post-copy line count check for knowledge.jsonl integrity

## Specific Changes Per Installer

### install-claude.sh
1. Line 155: `mkdir -p "$TARGET/.beads/config"` → `mkdir -p "$TARGET/.lavra/config"` (belt-and-suspenders)
2. Lines 474-475: Update messaging to .lavra/memory/knowledge.jsonl and .lavra/memory/recall.sh
3. Add: Call `migrate_beads_to_lavra "$TARGET"` AFTER provision_memory_dir call

### install-opencode.sh, install-gemini.sh, install-cortex.sh
Same three changes as install-claude.sh for each.

### uninstall-claude.sh, uninstall-cortex.sh (lines 13-14, 224-225, 230)
1. Update messaging from .beads/ → .lavra/ (what Lavra data is preserved)
2. Add: "To fully remove Lavra data: rm -rf .lavra/"

### uninstall-opencode.sh (lines 131-143), uninstall-gemini.sh (lines 110-122)
These remove recall.sh, knowledge-db.sh, and optionally knowledge.jsonl from .beads/memory/.
Update all paths to .lavra/memory/.

### scripts/test-installation.sh, scripts/test-features.sh
Update all path assertions:
- .beads/config/lavra.json → .lavra/config/lavra.json
- .beads/memory/.gitignore → .lavra/.gitignore (now at root)
- .beads/memory/knowledge.jsonl → .lavra/memory/knowledge.jsonl
test-features.sh has ~20 references — update all.

## Decisions

### Locked
- Copy + warn (not delete, not interactive prompt)
- Do NOT touch .beads/ issue/comment/backup data
- Migration fires automatically on install if .beads/memory/knowledge.jsonl detected
- SQLite files excluded from copy (may have open WAL state)
- Function name: `migrate_beads_to_lavra` (no leading underscore)
- Post-copy line count integrity check required

### Discretion
- Exact warning message wording (example above is a good default)
- Whether to print per-file migration details

## Testing
- [ ] Fresh install: .lavra/memory/ created, no .beads/memory/ written by Lavra
- [ ] Migration install: pre-populate .beads/memory/knowledge.jsonl, run installer, verify .lavra/memory/knowledge.jsonl has same line count
- [ ] Re-install test: run installer twice — second run skips migration (already has data)
- [ ] Config migration: lavra.json, project-setup.md, codebase-profile.md copied
- [ ] Retro migration: .beads/retros/2026-01-01.json appears in .lavra/retros/
- [ ] SQLite files (.db, .db-wal) NOT in .lavra/memory/ after migration
- [ ] old recall.sh from .beads/ NOT overwriting new .lavra/memory/recall.sh (cp -n protects)
- [ ] .beads/backup/ NOT touched
- [ ] Line count check warning fires when migration is incomplete
- [ ] `grep -r "\.beads/memory" installers/` returns zero results

## Validation
- [ ] `grep -r "\.beads/memory" installers/` returns only migration-detection code
- [ ] Warning message printed when .beads/memory/knowledge.jsonl detected
- [ ] No warning when fresh install
- [ ] .beads/ data preserved after migration
- [ ] test-installation.sh and test-features.sh pass with new paths

## Files
- installers/install-claude.sh (primary)
- installers/install-opencode.sh
- installers/install-gemini.sh
- installers/install-cortex.sh
- installers/uninstall-claude.sh
- installers/uninstall-opencode.sh
- installers/uninstall-gemini.sh
- installers/uninstall-cortex.sh
- scripts/test-installation.sh
- scripts/test-features.sh

## Dependencies
- bdcompound-63c.1 (Phase 2) — hooks must target .lavra/ before installer runs

## References
- Security review: TARGET must be validated (absolute path, no traversal) before any file ops
- Data migration review: SQLite files excluded, .gitattributes/.gitignore excluded, integrity check required
- Deployment review: mkdir -p for retros destination before cp -rn; line count comparison
- Architecture review: cp -rn needs pre-flight check for existing .lavra/ data (idempotent)
- Pattern review: function name migrate_beads_to_lavra (no underscore prefix)
- Simplicity review: use per-file explicit exclusions rather than rsync (no extra dependency)"}

{"id":"bdcompound-63c.3","title":"Phase 3: Installer migration — copy .beads/ data to .lavra/","description":"## What
Update all installer and uninstaller scripts to provision .lavra/ instead of .beads/memory/, and add auto-migration logic that copies existing .beads/ Lavra data to .lavra/ on detection. Covers Claude, OpenCode, Gemini, and Cortex installers.

## Context
The installers live in installers/. Each platform installer calls provision-memory.sh (already updated in Phase 1) to set up the memory system. The installer also creates config directories, prints messaging, and handles the uninstall side. The new migration logic must detect if existing Lavra data lives in .beads/ and copy it to .lavra/ before proceeding.

## Migration Logic (for all installers)

Detection and copy block to add to each installer (after provision-memory.sh call):

```bash
# Migrate existing .beads/ Lavra data to .lavra/ if present
_migrate_beads_to_lavra() {
  local TARGET="$1"
  local migrated=0

  if [ -d "$TARGET/.beads/memory" ] && [ -f "$TARGET/.beads/memory/knowledge.jsonl" ]; then
    cp -rn "$TARGET/.beads/memory/." "$TARGET/.lavra/memory/" 2>/dev/null || true
    migrated=1
  fi

  if [ -d "$TARGET/.beads/config" ] && [ -f "$TARGET/.beads/config/lavra.json" ]; then
    cp -rn "$TARGET/.beads/config/lavra.json" "$TARGET/.lavra/config/" 2>/dev/null || true
    # Only copy lavra-owned config files, not beads-owned ones
    [ -f "$TARGET/.beads/config/project-setup.md" ] && cp -n "$TARGET/.beads/config/project-setup.md" "$TARGET/.lavra/config/" 2>/dev/null || true
    [ -f "$TARGET/.beads/config/codebase-profile.md" ] && cp -n "$TARGET/.beads/config/codebase-profile.md" "$TARGET/.lavra/config/" 2>/dev/null || true
    migrated=1
  fi

  if [ -d "$TARGET/.beads/retros" ]; then
    cp -rn "$TARGET/.beads/retros/." "$TARGET/.lavra/retros/" 2>/dev/null || true
    migrated=1
  fi

  if [ "$migrated" -eq 1 ]; then
    echo ""
    echo "[!] Old Lavra data found in .beads/ — copied to .lavra/"
    echo "    .beads/memory/, .beads/config/ (Lavra files), .beads/retros/ preserved."
    echo "    Safe to delete after verifying .lavra/ data is intact."
    echo ""
  fi
}
```

Notes on migration logic:
- Use `cp -rn` (no-clobber) to not overwrite existing .lavra/ files if already present
- Only copy Lavra-owned config files (lavra.json, project-setup.md, codebase-profile.md) — NOT beads-owned files
- Do NOT copy .beads/backup/ (owned by beads CLI)
- Do NOT delete .beads/ originals (user deletes after verification)
- Call _migrate_beads_to_lavra AFTER provision-memory.sh runs (so .lavra/ dirs exist)

## Specific Changes Per Installer

### uninstall-opencode.sh, uninstall-gemini.sh, uninstall-cortex.sh
Update references from .beads/memory/recall.sh and .beads/memory/knowledge-db.sh to .lavra/ paths (lines 131-143 in opencode, 110-122 in gemini).

### scripts/test-installation.sh and scripts/test-features.sh
Update all path checks from .beads/config/ and .beads/memory/ to .lavra/ equivalents.

## Decisions

### Locked
- Copy + warn (not delete, not interactive prompt)
- Do NOT touch .beads/ issue/comment/backup data — only Lavra-owned files
- Migration fires automatically on install if .beads/memory/knowledge.jsonl detected
- Use cp -rn (no-clobber) to be safe on re-installs

### Discretion
- Exact warning message wording
- Whether to print migration details (which files were copied) or just a summary line

## Testing

## Files

{"description":"## What
Update all command .md files, optional commands, agent .md files, skill files, AND platform-converted copies (opencode/, gemini/, cortex/) to reference .lavra/ paths instead of .beads/ paths.

## Context
Commands live in plugins/lavra/commands/ and plugins/lavra/commands/optional/. Agents in plugins/lavra/agents/. Skills in plugins/lavra/skills/.
CRITICAL: platform-converted copies exist in plugins/lavra/opencode/, plugins/lavra/gemini/, plugins/lavra/cortex/ — 82+ files total with .beads/ refs. These MUST be updated too.

Files contain .beads/ path references in:
1. Shell commands users/agents run (e.g., `.beads/memory/recall.sh "keyword"`)
2. Configuration references (e.g., `cat .beads/config/lavra.json`)
3. Session state writes (e.g., `.beads/memory/session-state.md`)
4. Protected artifact lists (e.g., `.beads/memory/, .beads/config/` in "do not modify" sections)
5. XML tag attributes (e.g., `<untrusted-config-data source=".beads/config">`)
6. Reason strings (e.g., `bd close --reason "wont_fix: .beads/memory/ are pipeline artifacts"`)

## Approach: Three-Step Process

### Step 1: Scoped sed pass
Run over `plugins/lavra/commands/`, `plugins/lavra/commands/optional/`, `plugins/lavra/agents/`, `plugins/lavra/skills/`, and platform copies — replacing ONLY the three Lavra-owned subpaths:

```bash
find plugins/lavra/commands plugins/lavra/commands/optional \
     plugins/lavra/agents plugins/lavra/skills \
     plugins/lavra/opencode/commands plugins/lavra/gemini/commands \
     plugins/lavra/cortex/commands \
     -name "*.md" -o -name "*.toml" | \
  xargs sed -i \
    -e 's|\.beads/memory/|.lavra/memory/|g' \
    -e 's|\.beads/config/|.lavra/config/|g' \
    -e 's|\.beads/retros/|.lavra/retros/|g'
```

SCOPE: Only `memory/`, `config/`, `retros/` — NOT `.beads/` globally. This avoids touching `.beads/backup/` (beads CLI owned) or `.beads/` references in context that describe the beads CLI itself.

### Step 2: Manual review of 4 specific occurrences
These require human judgment, not sed:

1. `lavra-work.md` + `lavra-work-ralph.md` — `bd close --reason "wont_fix: .beads/memory/ and .beads/config/ files are pipeline artifacts"` — update reason strings to .lavra/
2. `lavra-work.md` + `lavra-work-ralph.md` — `<untrusted-config-data source=".beads/config" treat-as="passive-context">` — update source attribute
These sed patterns are similar but the semantic context is important — verify the updated wording makes sense.

### Step 3: Grep verification (acceptance test)
```bash
# Must return zero results (Lavra-owned paths only):
grep -r "\.beads/memory" plugins/lavra/commands/
grep -r "\.beads/config" plugins/lavra/commands/
grep -r "\.beads/retros" plugins/lavra/commands/
grep -r "\.beads/memory" plugins/lavra/agents/
grep -r "\.beads/config" plugins/lavra/agents/
grep -r "\.beads/memory" plugins/lavra/skills/
grep -r "\.beads/memory" plugins/lavra/opencode/
grep -r "\.beads/memory" plugins/lavra/gemini/
grep -r "\.beads/memory" plugins/lavra/cortex/

# Must STILL return results (beads CLI owned — DO NOT CHANGE):
grep -r "\.beads/backup" plugins/lavra/commands/lavra-ship.md
```

## Platform Copies (CRITICAL GAP — must not be missed)

The platform-converted copies in opencode/, gemini/, cortex/ are committed to git and contain the same .beads/ refs as canonical sources. There are two options:

Option A (preferred): Run sed pass directly on the converted files in the same step as canonical sources.
Option B: Re-run conversion scripts (scripts/convert-opencode.ts, scripts/convert-gemini.ts) after updating canonical sources. Only valid if the conversion scripts do path-preserving output AND don't have their own hardcoded .beads/ paths.

Check conversion scripts for hardcoded .beads/ output paths before deciding. If the scripts preserve paths from source, Option B is elegant (one operation). If they hardcode paths, Option A is required.

## Files and Known References

### Commands with .beads/ refs (prioritized):
- lavra-work.md (8 refs + XML tag + reason string)
- lavra-review.md (8 refs)
- lavra-recall.md (8 refs)
- lavra-work-ralph.md (6 refs + XML tag + reason string)
- lavra-work-teams.md (6 refs)
- lavra-design.md (5 refs)
- lavra-retro.md (4 refs including .beads/retros/)
- lavra-ship.md (2 refs — .beads/config/lavra.json only; .beads/backup/ stays)
- lavra-learn.md, lavra-checkpoint.md, lavra-brainstorm.md, lavra-plan.md, lavra-eng-review.md, lavra-research.md, lavra-quick.md (1-2 refs each)

### Agents with .beads/ refs:
- agents/research/learnings-researcher.md: .beads/memory/knowledge.jsonl, .beads/memory/knowledge.archive.jsonl, .beads/memory/recall.sh
- agents/research/git-history-analyzer.md: .beads/memory/ (protected artifacts list)
- agents/review/code-simplicity-reviewer.md: .beads/memory/, .beads/config/ (protected artifacts list)

### Skills with .beads/ refs:
- skills/lavra-knowledge/references/jsonl-schema.md (in references/ subdir — grep must include subdirs)
- Check all skills dirs including optional/

## Decisions

### Locked
- .beads/backup/ references are NOT changed (beads CLI ownership)
- Historical releases/v*.md files are NOT changed
- Platform copies (opencode/, gemini/, cortex/) MUST be updated in same phase

### Discretion
- Option A vs B for platform copies (check conversion scripts first)
- Whether to do one combined sed pass or file-by-file

## Testing
- [ ] All grep verification commands above return zero results
- [ ] .beads/backup/ in lavra-ship.md still present (not changed)
- [ ] Manual spot-check: lavra-work.md — reason string and XML tag updated
- [ ] Manual spot-check: learnings-researcher.md — knowledge.jsonl path updated
- [ ] Platform copies spot-check: opencode/commands/lavra-retro.md references .lavra/retros/
- [ ] Gemini conversion: gemini/commands/lavra-retro.toml references .lavra/retros/

## Validation
- [ ] All grep checks pass
- [ ] .beads/backup/ in lavra-ship.md preserved
- [ ] Platform copies (all three platforms) updated
- [ ] skills/lavra-knowledge/references/ subdirs checked

## Files
- plugins/lavra/commands/*.md (all core commands)
- plugins/lavra/commands/optional/*.md (all optional commands)
- plugins/lavra/agents/**/*.md (3 agents with refs)
- plugins/lavra/skills/**/*.md and SKILL.md files
- plugins/lavra/opencode/commands/*.md (platform copy)
- plugins/lavra/gemini/commands/*.toml (platform copy)
- plugins/lavra/cortex/commands/*.md (platform copy)
- scripts/convert-opencode.ts (check for hardcoded output paths)

{"description":"## What
Run full end-to-end smoke tests covering fresh install, migration (upgrade from 0.7.0), and all platform installers. Perform final grep validation, bump version to 0.8.0, and release.

## Context
This is the final validation gate. All previous phases must be complete. This phase adds two scenarios not in the original scope: the migration (upgrade) smoke test and post-migration data integrity verification.

## Final Grep Validation (run first — must all return zero)
```bash
# Lavra-owned paths — must be zero:
grep -r "\.beads/memory" plugins/lavra/
grep -r "\.beads/config" plugins/lavra/
grep -r "\.beads/retros" plugins/lavra/
grep -r "\.beads/memory" installers/
grep -r "\.beads/config" installers/
grep -r "\.beads/memory" .claude/rules/
grep -r "\.beads/memory" site/src/content/docs/
grep -r "\.beads/config" site/src/content/docs/

# Allowed exceptions (must still return results):
grep "\.beads/backup" plugins/lavra/commands/lavra-ship.md  # beads CLI owned
grep "\.beads" site/src/content/docs/releases/              # historical
# Migration detection code in installers (searching FOR .beads/memory/knowledge.jsonl)
```

## Smoke Test Matrix

### Scenario 1: Claude — Fresh Install
```bash
mkdir -p /tmp/test-lavra-fresh && cd /tmp/test-lavra-fresh
git init -q && bd init --quiet 2>/dev/null
bash ~/Documents/projects/lavra/install.sh /tmp/test-lavra-fresh
```

Verify .lavra/ ROOT structure:
```bash
ls /tmp/test-lavra-fresh/.lavra/
# Must contain: .gitignore, .gitattributes, .lavra-version, memory/, config/, retros/

cat /tmp/test-lavra-fresh/.lavra/.gitignore
# Must contain: memory/knowledge.db (paths use memory/ prefix)

cat /tmp/test-lavra-fresh/.lavra/.gitattributes
# Must contain: memory/knowledge.jsonl merge=union

cat /tmp/test-lavra-fresh/.lavra/.lavra-version
# Must contain: 0.8.0

ls /tmp/test-lavra-fresh/.lavra/memory/
# Must contain: knowledge.jsonl, recall.sh, knowledge-db.sh
# Must NOT contain: .db, .db-wal (gitignored; not staged)

# Must NOT have .beads/memory/ created by Lavra
[ -d /tmp/test-lavra-fresh/.beads/memory ] && echo "FAIL" || echo "PASS"
```

git merge=union verification:
```bash
cd /tmp/test-lavra-fresh
git check-attr merge .lavra/memory/knowledge.jsonl
# Expected: .lavra/memory/knowledge.jsonl: merge: union
```

### Scenario 2: Claude — Migration (Upgrade from 0.7.0)
```bash
mkdir -p /tmp/test-lavra-migrate && cd /tmp/test-lavra-migrate
git init -q && bd init --quiet 2>/dev/null

# Simulate existing 0.7.0 Lavra data in .beads/
mkdir -p .beads/memory .beads/config .beads/retros
echo '{"key":"test-key","type":"learned","content":"Test knowledge","tags":["test"],"ts":1000000}' > .beads/memory/knowledge.jsonl
echo '{"workflow":{"research":true,"plan_review":true}}' > .beads/config/lavra.json
echo '{"date":"2026-01-01","items":[]}' > .beads/retros/2026-01-01.json
LINES_BEFORE=$(wc -l < .beads/memory/knowledge.jsonl | tr -d ' ')

bash ~/Documents/projects/lavra/install.sh /tmp/test-lavra-migrate
```

Verify migration:
```bash
# .lavra/ has migrated data
LINES_AFTER=$(wc -l < /tmp/test-lavra-migrate/.lavra/memory/knowledge.jsonl | tr -d ' ')
echo "Before: $LINES_BEFORE, After: $LINES_AFTER"
[ "$LINES_BEFORE" -eq "$LINES_AFTER" ] && echo "PASS: line counts match" || echo "FAIL: line mismatch"

# Knowledge entry preserved
grep "test-key" /tmp/test-lavra-migrate/.lavra/memory/knowledge.jsonl && echo "PASS" || echo "FAIL"

# Config migrated
cat /tmp/test-lavra-migrate/.lavra/config/lavra.json

# Retros migrated
ls /tmp/test-lavra-migrate/.lavra/retros/

# .beads/ originals preserved (NOT deleted)
[ -f /tmp/test-lavra-migrate/.beads/memory/knowledge.jsonl ] && echo "PASS: .beads/ intact" || echo "FAIL: .beads/ deleted"

# Warning printed during install
bash ~/Documents/projects/lavra/install.sh /tmp/test-lavra-migrate 2>&1 | grep "Old Lavra data"
```

Verify SQLite NOT migrated:
```bash
[ -f /tmp/test-lavra-migrate/.lavra/memory/knowledge.db ] && echo "FAIL: SQLite copied" || echo "PASS: no SQLite"
```

### Scenario 3: Claude — .lavra/ gitignore warning
```bash
echo ".lavra/" >> /tmp/test-lavra-fresh/.gitignore
bash ~/Documents/projects/lavra/install.sh /tmp/test-lavra-fresh 2>&1 | grep -A5 '\[!\] Warning'
# Must show data loss warning about .lavra/ (not .beads/)
```

### Scenario 4: OpenCode — Fresh Install
```bash
mkdir -p /tmp/test-lavra-opencode && cd /tmp/test-lavra-opencode
git init -q && bd init --quiet 2>/dev/null
bash ~/Documents/projects/lavra/install.sh -opencode --yes /tmp/test-lavra-opencode
```

Verify:
```bash
ls /tmp/test-lavra-opencode/.lavra/memory/
# Must contain: knowledge.jsonl, recall.sh, knowledge-db.sh

ls /tmp/test-lavra-opencode/.lavra/
# Must contain: .gitignore, .gitattributes, .lavra-version
```

### Scenario 5: Gemini — Fresh Install
```bash
mkdir -p /tmp/test-lavra-gemini && cd /tmp/test-lavra-gemini
git init -q && bd init --quiet 2>/dev/null
bash ~/Documents/projects/lavra/install.sh -gemini --yes /tmp/test-lavra-gemini
```

Verify same structure as OpenCode.

### Cleanup
```bash
rm -rf /tmp/test-lavra-fresh /tmp/test-lavra-migrate /tmp/test-lavra-opencode /tmp/test-lavra-gemini
cd ~/Documents/projects/lavra
```

## Version Bump
Update these 4 locations to 0.8.0 (current: 0.7.0):
1. plugins/lavra/.claude-plugin/plugin.json
2. .claude-plugin/marketplace.json
3. LAVRA_VERSION constant in plugins/lavra/hooks/auto-recall.sh
4. Version string in plugins/lavra/hooks/provision-memory.sh (the `echo "X.Y.Z"` line)

Verify consistency:
```bash
bash scripts/pre-release-check.sh
```

## Pre-Release Check
```bash
bash scripts/pre-release-check.sh
# Must pass with zero failures
```

## Decisions

### Locked
- Both fresh install AND migration scenarios must pass before tagging
- Version bump to 0.8.0 (significant structural change — minor version bump appropriate)
- All 4 version locations must match
- Migration smoke test (Scenario 2) is required — not optional

### Discretion
- Exact version (0.8.0 confirmed appropriate for structural directory change)

## Testing
All scenarios described in Smoke Test Matrix above.

## Validation
- [ ] All grep checks return zero results (with exceptions)
- [ ] Fresh install: .lavra/ root has .gitignore, .gitattributes, .lavra-version (NOT in .lavra/memory/)
- [ ] Fresh install: .lavra/memory/ has knowledge.jsonl, recall.sh, knowledge-db.sh
- [ ] Fresh install: git check-attr merge returns union for .lavra/memory/knowledge.jsonl
- [ ] Migration install: .lavra/ populated with .beads/ data, originals preserved, line counts match
- [ ] Migration install: SQLite files NOT in .lavra/memory/
- [ ] Migration warning message printed
- [ ] Re-install safe: second install doesn't overwrite existing .lavra/ data
- [ ] OpenCode installer creates .lavra/ structure correctly
- [ ] Gemini installer creates .lavra/ structure correctly
- [ ] .lavra/ gitignore warning fires when .lavra/ is in project .gitignore
- [ ] pre-release-check.sh passes
- [ ] All 4 version files consistent at 0.8.0

## Files

{"description":"## What
Update provision-memory.sh to create and manage a .lavra/ directory structure instead of writing into .beads/. Create a folder-level .lavra/.gitignore and .lavra/.gitattributes (NOT subdirectory-level). This is the foundation — all other phases depend on this structure being correct.

## Context
provision-memory.sh is at plugins/lavra/hooks/provision-memory.sh. It currently:
- Creates .beads/memory/ with knowledge.jsonl, recall.sh, knowledge-db.sh, .gitattributes, .gitignore, .lavra-version
- Creates .beads/config/ with lavra.json
- Runs git add on all .beads/memory/ files
- Validates project .gitignore for .beads/ patterns (warns if present)

The new structure moves ALL of this to .lavra/, with a single .gitignore and .gitattributes at .lavra/ root (not .lavra/memory/).

## New .lavra/ Directory Structure
```
.lavra/
├── .gitignore        (NEW: folder-level, replaces .beads/memory/.gitignore)
├── .gitattributes    (NEW: folder-level, replaces .beads/memory/.gitattributes)
├── .lavra-version    (MOVED: was .beads/memory/.lavra-version)
├── memory/
│   ├── knowledge.jsonl
│   ├── knowledge.archive.jsonl
│   ├── knowledge.db           (gitignored via .lavra/.gitignore)
│   ├── knowledge.db-journal   (gitignored)
│   ├── knowledge.db-wal       (gitignored)
│   ├── knowledge.db-shm       (gitignored)
│   ├── session-state.md       (gitignored)
│   ├── recall.sh
│   └── knowledge-db.sh
├── config/
│   ├── lavra.json
│   ├── project-setup.md       (committed when created)
│   └── codebase-profile.md    (committed when created)
└── retros/
    └── {date}.json            (committed)
```

## .lavra/.gitignore content (new, folder-level)
```
# SQLite FTS cache (rebuilt from knowledge.jsonl on first use)
memory/knowledge.db
memory/knowledge.db-journal
memory/knowledge.db-wal
memory/knowledge.db-shm

# Ephemeral session state (survives compaction, recalled once, then deleted)
memory/session-state.md
```
Note: paths are relative to .lavra/ root, so they use memory/ prefix.

## .lavra/.gitattributes content (new, folder-level)
```
memory/knowledge.jsonl merge=union
memory/knowledge.archive.jsonl merge=union
```
Note: CRITICAL — paths MUST include the memory/ prefix. bare 'knowledge.jsonl' would NOT match .lavra/memory/knowledge.jsonl. The gitattributes must say 'memory/knowledge.jsonl merge=union'.

## Decisions

### Locked
- Folder-level .gitignore and .gitattributes at .lavra/ root (NOT .lavra/memory/)
- .lavra-version marker lives at .lavra/.lavra-version (NOT .lavra/memory/)
- Hard path to .lavra/ — no .beads/ fallback
- .lavra/ contents committed to git; SQLite cache and session-state.md gitignored
- mkdir for .lavra/retros/ must also be created

### Discretion
- Exact wording of the printed output messages
- Whether to create knowledge.archive.jsonl as empty file or let it be created on first rotation

## Specific Changes to provision-memory.sh
1. Line 18: MEMORY_DIR path → .lavra/memory
2. Line 72: CONFIG_DIR path → .lavra/config
3. Do NOT add a LAVRA_DIR variable — use "$PROJECT_DIR/.lavra" inline at the two call sites. No new abstraction needed.
4. Add: mkdir -p "$PROJECT_DIR/.lavra/retros" in provisioning block
5. .gitattributes: write to "$PROJECT_DIR/.lavra/.gitattributes" (NOT MEMORY_DIR) with 'memory/knowledge.jsonl merge=union'
6. .gitignore: write to "$PROJECT_DIR/.lavra/.gitignore" (NOT MEMORY_DIR) with 'memory/knowledge.db' etc. (memory/ prefix paths)
7. .lavra-version: write to "$PROJECT_DIR/.lavra/.lavra-version" (NOT MEMORY_DIR)
8. Lines 97-107: Update gitignore validation — change regex from \.beads/ to \.lavra/. Update warning message text to say 'Lavra knowledge and config' (not 'beads issues, comments, and knowledge').
9. Lines 135-143: Update git add block — MUST stage .lavra/ paths:
   - .lavra/.gitattributes
   - .lavra/.gitignore
   - .lavra/.lavra-version
   - .lavra/memory/knowledge.jsonl
   - .lavra/memory/recall.sh
   - .lavra/memory/knowledge-db.sh
   REMOVE -f flag: use plain 'git add' (no -f) to respect user's .gitignore. The -f override can silently commit AI-generated knowledge (API keys, internal architecture notes) if user has .lavra/ gitignored in a private project.
10. Directory creation: use mkdir -p -m 700 for .lavra/memory/ to prevent world-readable permissions on knowledge.jsonl (may contain sensitive technical information). Apply same to config/ and retros/.
11. Update all echo messages to reference .lavra/ not .beads/

## Testing
- [ ] Run provision-memory.sh on fresh project — verify .lavra/ structure created correctly
- [ ] Verify .gitattributes at .lavra/ root (not .lavra/memory/), containing 'memory/knowledge.jsonl merge=union'
- [ ] Verify .gitignore at .lavra/ root, containing 'memory/knowledge.db' (with memory/ prefix)
- [ ] Verify .lavra-version at .lavra/ root (not .lavra/memory/)
- [ ] Run provision-memory.sh twice — should be idempotent (no duplicate files/dirs)
- [ ] Test gitignore validation: project with .lavra/ in .gitignore — confirm warning fires (not .beads/)
- [ ] Run 'git check-attr merge .lavra/memory/knowledge.jsonl' after install — must return 'merge: union'
- [ ] Verify git add stages .lavra/ files (git status shows .lavra/... tracked)
- [ ] Verify git add does NOT use -f flag (intentionally ignored .lavra/ should not be force-staged)

## Validation
- [ ] .lavra/.gitattributes content uses memory/ prefix: 'memory/knowledge.jsonl merge=union'
- [ ] .lavra/.gitignore content uses memory/ prefix: 'memory/knowledge.db'
- [ ] .lavra-version is at .lavra/ root, not .lavra/memory/
- [ ] git add block stages .lavra/ paths without -f flag
- [ ] No -f flag on git add (security: respect user's gitignore)
- [ ] mkdir uses -m 700 for .lavra/memory/, .lavra/config/, .lavra/retros/
- [ ] Version string '0.7.0' still matches LAVRA_VERSION in auto-recall.sh (just path changed)

## Files
- plugins/lavra/hooks/provision-memory.sh

## Dependencies
- None (this is the foundation phase)

## References
- Architecture review: git add -f staging block is highest-risk missed change
- Architecture review: .gitattributes memory/ prefix is critical for correct gitattribute scoping
- Security review: Remove -f from git add (M4) — can silently stage files user wants private
- Security review: Use mkdir -m 700 for memory directory (L2) — knowledge.jsonl may contain sensitive info
- Simplicity review: Do NOT add LAVRA_DIR variable — inline the path, no abstraction for local use"}

{"description":"## What
Recode all hook scripts to use .lavra/ paths instead of .beads/ paths. Three files change (auto-recall.sh, memory-capture.sh, check-memory.sh + recall.sh template). Two files need zero changes (subagent-wrapup.sh, knowledge-db.sh). Also fix three security issues and two silent-failure guard bugs in auto-recall.sh and check-memory.sh.

## Context
Hook scripts live in plugins/lavra/hooks/. After Phase 1 updates provision-memory.sh, these hooks still point to .beads/ paths and will fail. This phase updates the runtime hooks so the installed system works end-to-end.

Files that need changes:
- auto-recall.sh (SessionStart hook)
- memory-capture.sh (PostToolUse hook)
- check-memory.sh (optional check script)
- recall.sh (template search script, installed to .lavra/memory/)

Files that need ZERO changes:
- subagent-wrapup.sh — no .beads/ references (confirmed)
- knowledge-db.sh — all paths are passed as parameters, fully agnostic (confirmed)

## Specific Changes

### auto-recall.sh
- Lines 32-38: Guard 'if [ ! -d "$PROJECT_DIR/.beads" ]' — MUST change to check .lavra/ for non-beads projects. After migration, projects with .lavra/ but no .beads/ would always exit with the 'beads not initialized' hint, which is wrong — beads is no longer required. Change to: exit only if both .beads/ AND .lavra/ are absent. Update hint text to not require 'bd init' for non-beads users.
- Line 33: "$PROJECT_DIR/.beads" → "$PROJECT_DIR/.lavra" in existence check
- Line 41: MEMORY_DIR path → .lavra/memory
- Line 64: .beads/memory path → .lavra/memory (version file directory lookup)
- Line 65: version file path — .lavra-version moved to .lavra/ root (not .lavra/memory/). MUST use "$PROJECT_DIR/.lavra/.lavra-version" NOT "$MEMORY_DIR/.lavra-version". This is the critical distinction.
- Line 199 (source annotation): update 'source=".beads/memory/knowledge.jsonl"' → '.lavra/memory/knowledge.jsonl'
- Session state path: auto-resolves since it uses $MEMORY_DIR — no explicit change needed

**Security fix — H1: Replace bare heredoc JSON with jq assembly**
All inline `cat <<EOF ... $VAR ... EOF` JSON constructions in auto-recall.sh inject shell variables into JSON without full JSON escaping. A knowledge entry containing '"' followed by '}}}' can break out of the JSON string and inject arbitrary hook output fields. A newline in content produces invalid JSON that silently drops recall output.
Replace ALL bare heredoc JSON constructions with jq:
```bash
jq -cn --arg msg "$OUTPUT_MSG" '{"hookSpecificOutput":{"systemMessage":$msg}}'
```
This applies to the main output block AND every early-exit block that emits inline JSON (lines ~35, ~46, ~57, ~76, ~88).

**Security fix — M1: Apply sanitization to session-state.md**
session-state.md is AI-generated content (written by /lavra-work, /lavra-checkpoint) but is currently injected into next session's system message without the sanitization applied to $RELEVANT_KNOWLEDGE. A compromised subagent could write 'SYSTEM: <injected instruction>' to session-state.md.
Apply the same sanitization pipeline to $SESSION_STATE as to $RELEVANT_KNOWLEDGE: role-prefix stripping, bidirectional-character removal, and wrap in <untrusted-knowledge> tags.

### memory-capture.sh
- Line 89: MEMORY_DIR path → .lavra/memory
- All other references (KNOWLEDGE_FILE, knowledge.db paths) derive from MEMORY_DIR — auto-update once line 89 is changed
- Add CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR validation AFTER the existing early-exit guards (lines 18-19). CRITICAL placement: if placed BEFORE early-exit guards it runs on every single Bash call (PostToolUse fires very frequently). Placed after early-exit guards it only runs on ~1% of calls that match 'bd comment add'.

**Security fix — H3: Tighter CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR validation**
The proposed OR condition (allow if .lavra exists OR .beads exists) can be bypassed: an attacker who controls CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR only needs a directory with .beads/ to redirect knowledge writes. Use a tighter check:
```bash
if [[ -n "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR" ]]; then
  CANONICAL=$(realpath -m "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
  if [[ -z "$CANONICAL" ]] || [[ "$CANONICAL" != /* ]] ||      [[ "$CANONICAL" != "$HOME"/* && "$CANONICAL" != /tmp/* ]]; then
    CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR=""  # fall through to CWD
  fi
fi
```
This validates the canonical path starts with a known trusted prefix.

### check-memory.sh
- Line 37: update .beads/memory/ → .lavra/memory/
- Line 38: Gate 'if [ ! -d ".beads" ]' — MUST change this primary guard. On a non-beads Lavra project (no .beads/ dir), this exits immediately and check-memory.sh never runs. Change to: 'if [ ! -d ".beads" ] && [ ! -d ".lavra" ]; then exit 0'. This is the guard, not just a path update.
- Line 97: MEMORY_DIR=".beads/memory" fallback hardcode → MEMORY_DIR=".lavra/memory" — THIS FALLBACK must be caught. It's in the else branch for missing provision script.
  NOTE: Do NOT add CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR validation here — this is the broken-install fallback path. Just change the string.
  NOTE: check-memory.sh has a separate gate at line 38 for .beads existence — that guard is now architecturally incorrect (Lavra's memory doesn't need beads). Document this as a known limitation in a comment if the full fix is deferred, but the guard MUST be updated.

### recall.sh (template, gets installed to .lavra/memory/recall.sh)
- Lines 3, 14: update .beads/memory/knowledge.jsonl → .lavra/memory/knowledge.jsonl
  Note: recall.sh is installed BY VALUE (copied by provision-memory.sh) into the project at .lavra/memory/recall.sh. The template must be updated; re-running provision on an existing install will overwrite old copy with new one.

## Decisions

### Locked
- Hard recode — no dual-path fallback, no env var
- subagent-wrapup.sh and knowledge-db.sh are NOT modified
- .lavra-version file is at .lavra/ root (not .lavra/memory/) — auto-recall.sh must use PROJECT_DIR/.lavra/.lavra-version
- check-memory.sh line 38 guard MUST be updated (not just lines 37 and 97)
- CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR validation placement: AFTER early-exit guards in memory-capture.sh

### Discretion
- Whether to use realpath -m or readlink -f for CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR canonicalization (platform-dependent)
- Whether check-memory.sh gate fully switches to .lavra/ or uses the transition-safe AND condition

## Testing
- [ ] Fresh install: verify SessionStart hook fires and injects knowledge from .lavra/memory/knowledge.jsonl
- [ ] Run memory-capture.sh manually with a bd comment — verify entry appended to .lavra/memory/knowledge.jsonl
- [ ] Verify auto-recall.sh reads .lavra/.lavra-version (not .lavra/memory/.lavra-version)
- [ ] Verify session-state.md at .lavra/memory/session-state.md is sanitized and wrapped in <untrusted-knowledge>
- [ ] Test non-beads project: create .lavra/ without .beads/ — verify auto-recall.sh does NOT exit with 'beads not initialized'
- [ ] Test check-memory.sh on non-beads project with .lavra/ only — must not exit at line 38
- [ ] grep -r '\.beads/' plugins/lavra/hooks/ should return zero results (excluding migration script)
- [ ] Verify source annotation in auto-recall.sh output shows .lavra/memory/ path
- [ ] Verify JSON output from auto-recall.sh is valid JSON when knowledge contains '"' characters
- [ ] Verify CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR validation fires AFTER early-exit guards (check script flow)

## Validation
- [ ] No .beads/ references remain in auto-recall.sh, memory-capture.sh, check-memory.sh, recall.sh
- [ ] check-memory.sh line 38 guard updated (not just lines 37 and 97)
- [ ] check-memory.sh line 97 fallback updated (MEMORY_DIR=.lavra/memory)
- [ ] auto-recall.sh reads version from .lavra/.lavra-version (PROJECT_DIR/.lavra/, not MEMORY_DIR)
- [ ] auto-recall.sh JSON output uses jq assembly (no bare heredoc with variable interpolation)
- [ ] session-state.md content wrapped in <untrusted-knowledge> and sanitized
- [ ] CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR validation uses canonical path check (not just directory existence)
- [ ] CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR validation placed AFTER early-exit guards
- [ ] Non-beads projects: auto-recall.sh and check-memory.sh work without .beads/ present

## Files
- plugins/lavra/hooks/auto-recall.sh
- plugins/lavra/hooks/memory-capture.sh
- plugins/lavra/hooks/check-memory.sh
- plugins/lavra/hooks/recall.sh

## Dependencies
- bdcompound-63c.2 (Phase 1) must complete first

## References
- Architecture review: check-memory.sh line 38 guard and auto-recall.sh lines 32-38 guard are blocking for non-beads projects
- Architecture review: .lavra-version at .lavra/ root — use PROJECT_DIR/.lavra/.lavra-version in auto-recall.sh line 65
- Security review: H1 (JSON heredoc injection) — replace all cat <<EOF with jq assembly in auto-recall.sh
- Security review: H3 (CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR bypass) — use canonical path validation
- Security review: M1 (session-state.md bypasses sanitization) — wrap in untrusted-knowledge, apply sanitization
- Performance review: CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR validation MUST go after early-exit guards to avoid per-call overhead"}
